behavior_summary: When the player 'looks' at the bathroom-stuff item, the script reads the item's frame/state and shows a short label (a single bark) describing the sub-item currently represented by that frame (e.g., 'wash basin', 'water jug', 'towel', 'razor', 'soap', 'mirror').
After emitting the label the U8P_BATHSTUF::look process yields/suspends; there are no side effects, no global flags read or written, and no branching based on player input.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BATHSTUF::look is a process (processType 0x0208) triggered by the engine's U8P_BATHSTUF::look/inspect action.
Control is a simple frame-based dispatch: Item::getFrame(this) is compared against integer values (0..8) and the corresponding Bark text is shown via an if/else-if chain (switch-like).
Each branch issues a single Bark then calls suspend (process yield/end).
open_questions: Are the exact frame-to-label mappings (0='wash basin', 1='water jug', 2/3/4='towel', 5='razor', 6/7='soap', 8='mirror') authoritative, or derived solely from bark samples? (inferred from text samples; confidence medium)
Does suspend here always end the U8P_BATHSTUF::look process immediately, or merely yield to allow other engine activity? (suspend is inferred from raw nodes; confidence medium)

main_function.name: U8P_BATHSTUF::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short, player-facing label for the bathroom-stuff item based on its current frame/state when the player examines it.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player uses the U8P_BATHSTUF::look/examine action on this item (processType 0x0208).
Selection of which label to show depends on Item::getFrame(this) equal to a small integer index (0..8).

function_map.name: U8P_BATHSTUF::look
function_map.role: Look/inspect handler that selects and displays a short label based on item frame

function_map.name: Item::getFrame
function_map.role: Query the item's current frame/state index used to pick the label

function_map.name: Bark
function_map.role: Emit a short player-facing description string

function_map.name: suspend
function_map.role: Yield or pause the U8P_BATHSTUF::look process after showing the label

external_calls.purpose: Obtain the item's frame/state index to decide which label to display
Present the short descriptive text corresponding to the current frame
Yield or end the U8P_BATHSTUF::look process after the bark; inferred from suspend nodes

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: When the player 'looks' at the bathroom-stuff item, the script reads the item's frame/state and shows a short label (a single bark) describing the sub-item currently represented by that frame (e.g., 'wash basin', 'water jug', 'towel', 'razor', 'soap', 'mirror').
